videos.title: Estimation and Rounding Numbers | Grade 4 Math
videos.description: Learn how to estimate and round numbers to the nearest ten, hundred, and thousand. Use estimation to check answers and make quick calculations in everyday life.
learningObjectives: Understand why estimation is useful in everyday life
Round numbers to the nearest ten, hundred, and thousand
Use estimation to check if an answer is reasonable
keyConceptsCovered: What is estimation and why we use it
Rounding rules (5 or more rounds up, less than 5 rounds down)

videos.title: Comparing and Ordering Numbers up to 5 Digits
videos.description: Learn to compare 4 and 5 digit numbers using greater than, less than, and equal to signs. Also practice arranging numbers in ascending and descending order.
learningObjectives: Review comparing numbers to support estimation skills
Understand number magnitude for better estimates
keyConceptsCovered: Understanding which numbers are close together

videos.title: Place Value up to 5 Digits | Ten Thousands Place
videos.description: Learn about place value in 5-digit numbers. Understand ones, tens, hundreds, thousands, and ten thousands places with real-life examples like distances and populations!
learningObjectives: Review place value to support rounding decisions
Identify which digit to look at when rounding to a specific place
keyConceptsCovered: Identifying the rounding digit and the digit to its right
followUpTopics: Rounding to nearest ten, hundred, thousand

videos.title: Expanded Form of Large Numbers
videos.description: Understand expanded form of 4 and 5 digit numbers. Break numbers like 45,328 into 40,000 + 5,000 + 300 + 20 + 8 and learn how each digit has a different value.
learningObjectives: Use expanded form to understand why rounding works
See how each place value contributes to the total
keyConceptsCovered: Expanded form shows the value of each digit
Connection between expanded form and estimation

excellent_performance.description: For students who scored >= 90% on readiness
excellent_performance.rationale: Estimation and comparison for strong students

ready_performance.description: For students who scored 80-89% on readiness
ready_performance.rationale: Complete estimation coverage with place value support

partial_readiness.description: For students who scored 50-79% on readiness
partial_readiness.rationale: Build from place value through comparison to estimation

after_prerequisite_review.description: For students who scored < 50% but completed prerequisites

questions.question: Round 47,382 to the nearest thousand.
questions.explanation: Look at the hundreds digit (3). Since 3 < 5, we round down. 47,382 rounded to the nearest thousand is 47,000.

usageNotes.teacherGuidance: Use real-world contexts for estimation: estimating crowd sizes, distances between cities, prices of items. Ask students to round first and then check with the exact calculation.
usageNotes.parentGuidance: When shopping, ask your child to estimate the total bill by rounding each price to the nearest ten or hundred. Compare the estimate with the actual total.
usageNotes.studentGuidance: Estimation is like making a smart guess! When you round numbers, you make them simpler so you can do maths faster in your head. It is a superpower for everyday life!
